calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Franceschini for Teresa Cron (Administrator of 2 estates that are beneficiaries).
Gray Rothstein for Diocese of Davenport.
Bobby Lydon-Lam for Union Bank.
Peter Myers for and with Robert Mantz.

Mr. Lydon-Lam and Mr. Franceschini stated that they waive notice on behalf of their Clients. All Counsel indicated that nobody is in opposition. Mr. Myers stated that he is not aware of anybody opposing this and that he had received several calls, but no objections. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release (subject to receipt and review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release
Off Calendar Apr-16-2019 Continued To May-28-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jan-02-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Diocese of Davenport, Iowa.
Scott Grossman for John Collins and Thomas Collins.
Connie Yi for Union Bank (Trustee).
Paul Gruwell for Teresa Cron.
Olena Likhachova for Charles Schwedes and John Schwedes (Claimants).
Peter Myers for Robert Mantz (Executor of the Estate of Timothy Collins.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the Trust would have to terminate as to perpetuity, whether or not there is intestacy as a result of the deaths of the Decedent's Grandchildren, interpretation of the Los Angeles Court order, interpretation of language in the Trust instrument, what the Trustor's intent was, who the intestate heirs are, etc. The Petition for Instructions, Petition for Determination of Final Beneficiary and the Petition for Order Construing A Trust Instrument, etc. are taken under submission. The parties are to provide proposed orders.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Continued To Oct-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Continued To Oct-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Diocese of Davenport.
Connie Yi for Union Bank (Trustee).
Peter Myers for and with Robert Mantz (Personal Representative of the Estate of Timothy Collins).
Scott Grossman appeared by telephone for Thomas Collins and John Collins.

Ms. Yi stated that they are still continuing to do a search for Barbara Drake, that they are about 98% done, that she is confident the search will be completed by next week and that the search is complete except for one person for whom they do not have an address. Mr. Grossman stated that Thomas Collins passed away two weeks ago, that he needs to do a substitution of parties, that the Diocese's petition in Los Angeles was dismissed, and that he can do a supplement or amend the petition if required. Mr. Rothstein stated that the Diocese withdrew its petition, that the Los Angeles Court does not have a mechanism to reflect that and that no determination was made by the Los Angeles Court. Mr. Myers suggested the parties submit a stipulation of facts as to dates of death of the original Trustors and the grandchildren, and submit any argument anyone may have. Mr. Grossman stated that he agrees with Mr. Myers' suggestion. The Court stated that it will not act on the Trustee's petition unless the Trustee decides to amend it. All matters are continued to October 3, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow final potential heirs to be noticed, the parties to submit proposed facts, Mr. Grossman to file his final pleading and for any additional written argument. Any further pleadings are to be filed no later than September 22, 2017. The Court will take the matter under submission on that date with or without further argument.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Order Construing A Trust Instrument And Ascertaining Beneficiaries And Determining To Whom Property Shall Pass Upon Final Termination Of The Trust
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Continued To Oct-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-09-2017 Continued To Aug-01-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Diocese of Davenport.
Connie Yi for Union Bank.
Peter Myers for Jeff Minear and Robert Mantz.
Scott Grossman appeared by telephone for Thomas Collins and John Collins.

The Court stated that there are service issues. Mr. Myers stated that they found three post-deceased Grandchildren who were the Heirs, that their Estates are in Kitsap County, Marin County and Santa Clara County, that Theresa Crone is the representative of two of those Estates and is represented by Attorney Rick Franceschini, and that Mr. Franceschini indicated that he has filed a joinder. Mr. Rothstein stated that he found out about Ms. Crone at the hearing in Los Angeles, that the hearing on the Nunc Pro Tunc issue in Los Angeles is continued to June 30, 2017 and that the issue is with verbiage of the order that established the Trust. Ms. Yi stated that the heir search company has found another Grand Niece. The Petition for Instructions, Petition to Determine Final Beneficiary and Petition for Order Construing Trust Instrument, etc. are continued to August 1, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that there needs to be an amendment where the Trustee has to tell the Court what should happen.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Order Construing A Trust Instrument And Ascertaining Beneficiaries And Determining To Whom Property Shall Pass Upon Final Termination Of The Trust
Off Calendar May-09-2017 Continued To Aug-01-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Final Beneficiary
Off Calendar May-09-2017 Continued To Aug-01-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Continued To May-09-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Diocesse of Davenport.
Connie Yi for Union Bank.
Lily Moallem for and with Jeff Minear and specially appearing on behalf of Attorney Rick Franchescini for Teresa Cron.
Scott Grossman appeared by telephone for Thomas Collins and John Collins.

Mr. Rothstein stated that they had mediation, but it was unsuccessful, that there are some Estates that have to be re-opened for the Heirs to receive notice, that the Diocese will ask Los Angeles County to continue that matter to April 24, 2017 and requested a continuance on this matter to determine which order this Court will be operating on. Ms. Moallem stated that two of the Estates have been opened and that they are waiting for the last probate in Washington to open in mid February. The Petition for Instructions, the Petition to Determine Final Beneficiary and the Petition for Order Construing a Trust Instrument, etc. are continued to May 9, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Order Construing A Trust Instrument And Ascertaining Beneficiaries And Determining To Whom Property Shall Pass Upon Final Termination Of The Trust
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Continued To Feb-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Final Beneficiary
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Continued To Feb-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-06-2016 Continued To Dec-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for the Diocese of Davenport. Connie Yi for Union Bank. Scott Grossman (via court call) for (petitioners) Thomas & John Collins. Peter Myers for (heir) Jeffrey Minear.

The Court stated that there are various petitions to figure out what to do with the trust, that the parties are proposing mediation and that there seems to be potential heirs who did not receive notice as well as post deceased administrators. Mr. Myers stated that they are in touch with the parties in the Minear family and that Mr. Grossman is in touch with the Collins heirs, that they propose that they would enter into settlement and that the non noticed heirs could sign on to the settlement, that they doubt that the people who are 1/64th beneficiaries and living out of state would want to participate in mediation. The Court asked if there are personal representatives that have been appointed for the post deceased siblings. Mr. Grossman responded yes in regards to John Collins, that regarding the Ciske descendants they have received notice but have not appeared. The Court stated that it is possible that some of the people could band together and participate, that it would like to see notice given as broadly as possible prior to mediation, that how this comes out is not at all clear or the way people think it should be and that it is inclined to look at an order that builds into a period of time pre-mediation to give notice to everyone. Mr. Myers stated that it can be added and that the order stated mediation was to be complete by 10/31/16. The Court stated that to the extent that 1 sibling does not have a personal representative, what are they to do with the entitlement and that it may need a personal representative. Mr. Myers stated that they will run into notice issue. The court stated that they need consent of those that have an interest, that at least 2 heirs have gotten notice from the trustee but not of the Collins petition and that now presumably everyone is on the service list. Mr. Myers stated that there are blank lines on the order for the next hearing date and that he suggests the end of November. The Court stated that it is concerned that that will not be enough time. The Court continued the Petition for Instructions, the Petition for Determination of Final Beneficiary and the Petition for Order Construing a Trust Instrument and Ascertaining Beneficiaries, etc., to December 13, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 stating that notice is to be given to everyone, that the trust petition has procedural issues and notice issues with the estate. Mr. Myers stated that mediation may bring the others out.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

Petition For Determination Final Beneficiary
Off Calendar Jun-14-2016 Continued To Jul-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Connie Yi for petitioner MFUG Union Bank; Peter Myers for objector Jeff Minear; Scott Grossman (by CourtCall) for Thomas and John Collins

Ms. Yi reported that the Diocese of Davenport is going to engage counsel to represent its interests. She further stated that petitioner is continuing its search for intestate heirs. She requested that the matter be continued for 90 days. Mr. Myers atated that 90 days sounded right. Mr. Grossman also agreed that a continuance was needed. The Court responded that he would continue the matter, but that 90 days was too long. The matter was continued to July 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)

Petition For Determination Final Beneficiary
Off Calendar Apr-19-2016 Continued To Jun-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Connie Yi for (petitioner) Union Bank. Dwight Donovan for (respondent) Jeffrey Minear. Scott Grossman (via court call) for (petitioners) Thomas & John Collins.

The Court stated this is a petition to determine the final beneficiary, as to whether the conditional beneficiaries should or should not participate, that there is now no designated beneficiaries, that the court needs a response to the notes provided, that no notice has been given to the heirs or the daughter in law, that the petition does not provide the administrator's request for a specific outcome and that the matter will need to be continued. Ms. Yi stated that there has been a change of trust officers at the bank, that all of the heirs have been noticed, that the daughter in law is deceased and that they would like time for the parties to meet and confer. The Court asked how long of a continuance would be needed and if they would like to mediate or try to resolve the issues on their own. Mr. Donovan stated that the Archdiocese has been noticed but not taken a position. Ms. Yi stated that they would like a continuance to mid June to work things out and that from talking to the family they want it to go to the charity. The petition for determination of final beneficiary was continued to June 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to cure the procedural problems and to file an amended petition if necessary.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
